title: How to Think
summary: A simple 3-step process for every move

steps.title: Three Questions
steps.text: Before every move, run through this checklist: (1) What is my opponent threatening? (2) What are MY checks, captures, and threats? (3) If I play my candidate, what's the best reply? Answer all three, then move.

steps.title: The Blunder Check
steps.text: Before you play your move, pause and ask: "After I make this move, is any of my pieces being attacked? Can my opponent give check? Can they capture something free?" This 5-second check saves most blunders.

steps.title: Candidate Moves
steps.text: Strong players don't calculate one move — they calculate 2-3 candidates and compare. Pick moves that develop a piece, improve a square, or create a threat. Reject random-looking moves early.
